problems.title: Minimum Difficulty of a Job Schedule

problems.title: Build Array Where You Can Find the Maximum Exactly K Comparisons

problems.title: Minimum Cost to Cut a Stick

problems.title: Number of Pairs Satisfying Inequality
problems.notes: SUPPLEMENTAL (added to close a coverage gap): Second ordered-prefix-counting problem via BIT.

problems.title: Count Number of Teams
problems.notes: SUPPLEMENTAL (added to close a coverage gap): Second ordered-statistics counting problem, more approachable difficulty than the existing Hard-only pair.

problems.title: Car Pooling
problems.notes: SUPPLEMENTAL (added to close a coverage gap): Second interval-booking-with-capacity problem, simpler than My Calendar I/II/III.

problems.title: Design a Stack With Increment Operation
problems.notes: SUPPLEMENTAL (added to close a coverage gap): Second versioned/snapshot-style design problem.

problems.title: Rectangle Area II
problems.notes: SUPPLEMENTAL (added to close a coverage gap): Second coordinate-compression + segment-tree range-design problem.

problems.title: Range Sum Query - Mutable
problems.notes: SUPPLEMENTAL (added to close a coverage gap): A simpler Fenwick/segment-tree prerequisite that should precede the harder Booking Concert Tickets problem already in this pattern.

problems.title: Design Underground System
problems.notes: SUPPLEMENTAL (added to close a coverage gap): Second O(1)-amortized hashmap-based design problem, complementing LRU Cache.

problems.title: Design Twitter
problems.notes: SUPPLEMENTAL (added to close a coverage gap): Second recency/frequency-aware design problem, complementing LFU Cache.

problems.title: Random Pick with Blacklist
problems.notes: SUPPLEMENTAL (added to close a coverage gap): Second probabilistic/ordered-structure design problem, complementing Design Skiplist.

problems.title: Rotate Image
problems.notes: SUPPLEMENTAL (added to close a coverage gap): In-place 90-degree rotation via transpose-then-reverse; the canonical in-place grid transform.

problems.title: Spiral Matrix
problems.notes: SUPPLEMENTAL (added to close a coverage gap): Walk the matrix in spiral order by shrinking four boundaries as you traverse.

problems.title: Set Matrix Zeroes
problems.notes: SUPPLEMENTAL (added to close a coverage gap): Use the first row/column as O(1) marker storage to zero rows/columns in place.

problems.title: Game of Life
problems.notes: SUPPLEMENTAL (added to close a coverage gap): Encode next-state in spare bits so the board updates simultaneously in place.

problems.title: Valid Sudoku
problems.notes: SUPPLEMENTAL (added to close a coverage gap): Single pass maintaining row/column/box hash sets to detect duplicates.

problems.title: Majority Element
problems.notes: SUPPLEMENTAL (added to close a coverage gap): Boyer-Moore voting: keep one candidate and a count, cancel opposing votes in a single pass.

problems.title: Majority Element II
problems.notes: SUPPLEMENTAL (added to close a coverage gap): Generalize Boyer-Moore to two candidates for the > n/3 majority, then verify.

problems.title: Valid Palindrome
problems.notes: SUPPLEMENTAL (added to close a coverage gap): Two converging pointers skip non-alphanumerics and compare case-insensitively.

problems.title: Merge Sorted Array
problems.notes: SUPPLEMENTAL (added to close a coverage gap): Merge in place from the back using two read pointers to avoid overwriting.

problems.title: Rotate Array
problems.notes: SUPPLEMENTAL (added to close a coverage gap): Rotate by k using three in-place reversals (whole, first k, rest).

problems.title: Pow(x, n)
problems.notes: SUPPLEMENTAL (added to close a coverage gap): Fast exponentiation by squaring: halve the exponent each step for O(log n).

problems.title: Encode and Decode Strings
problems.notes: SUPPLEMENTAL (added to close a coverage gap): Length-prefix each chunk so decoding is unambiguous regardless of content.

problems.title: Construct Binary Tree from Preorder and Inorder Traversal
problems.notes: SUPPLEMENTAL (added to close a coverage gap): Preorder gives the root; its index in inorder splits left/right subtrees recursively.

problems.title: Binary Search Tree Iterator
problems.notes: SUPPLEMENTAL (added to close a coverage gap): Controlled in-order traversal with an explicit stack for amortized O(1) next().

problems.title: Subtree of Another Tree
problems.notes: SUPPLEMENTAL (added to close a coverage gap): For each node test same-tree equality recursively against the target subtree.

problems.title: Path Sum III
problems.notes: SUPPLEMENTAL (added to close a coverage gap): DFS carrying a running prefix-sum hash map to count downward paths summing to target.

problems.title: Longest Valid Parentheses
problems.notes: SUPPLEMENTAL (added to close a coverage gap): Stack of indices (or two counters) to measure the longest matched run.

problems.title: Meeting Rooms
problems.notes: SUPPLEMENTAL (added to close a coverage gap): Sort by start and check adjacent intervals for any overlap.

problems.title: Surrounded Regions
problems.notes: SUPPLEMENTAL (added to close a coverage gap): Flood-fill border-connected regions first; everything unreached is captured.

problems.title: Reconstruct Itinerary
problems.notes: SUPPLEMENTAL (added to close a coverage gap): Hierholzer's algorithm: DFS the Eulerian path, appending nodes post-order.

problems.title: Triangle
problems.notes: SUPPLEMENTAL (added to close a coverage gap): Bottom-up min-path DP collapsing each row into the one below in O(n) space.

problems.title: Predict the Winner
problems.notes: SUPPLEMENTAL (added to close a coverage gap): Interval game DP: dp[i][j] is the best score margin the current player can force.

problems.title: Range Sum Query 2D - Immutable
problems.notes: SUPPLEMENTAL (added to close a coverage gap): 2-D prefix-sum table answers any submatrix sum in O(1) via inclusion-exclusion.

problems.title: Longest Duplicate Substring
problems.notes: SUPPLEMENTAL (added to close a coverage gap): Binary search the length with Rabin-Karp rolling-hash duplicate detection.

changelog.summary: Phase 5 curriculum data-quality sweep. F15: resolved duplicate/revisit problem slots with revisit_of markers (no deletions). F18: replaced the linear skill chain with a real 1-4 prerequisite DAG, reordered stages to interview priority, registered SK-IE-00 as a meta-skill, gated challenges behind reinforcements. F17: added matrix-simulation and Boyer-Moore voting skills plus missing interview classics (581 problems, 93 skills). F16: backfilled lc_id and source urls. F19: repaired wrong notes and skill descriptions, swapped SK-QP-06 primary to Find Median from Data Stream, removed the dead secondary_skill field, renamed section to source_section, fixed the changelog/version.

Corrected 24 skill descriptions in knowledge/skills.json (22 genuine mismatches + the 2 fixed during the split, out of 90 total skills) where the PDF-derived notes described a different technique than the skill's own name -- e.g. 'O(1) Cache Design' was described as 'In-place Reversal', 'Word-Break DP' as 'KMP/Rabin-Karp'. Each was rewritten by hand against the actual technique the skill name and primary problem represent. This does not change any problem's skill assignment, difficulty, or role -- descriptions only.

Skill-first migration on top of the existing repo fixes (CPX/Foundational stage linkage, 557-problem validation counts, flat progress schema): removed `pattern`/`module`/`estimated_minutes`/`interview_frequency`/per-problem `dependency`; added `primary_skill`, `secondary_skill`, `problem_role`, `difficulty_weight`, `importance`. Stages remapped to the 13 frozen reasoning stages backed by knowledge/skills.json. Split the conflated 'Invariant Scan / Greedy on Arrays' pattern into 'Running Extremum Scan' and 'Single-Pass Greedy Choice'; re-tagged Partition Labels and Min Arrows to Burst Balloons onto 'Interval / Scheduling Greedy' (Interval Reasoning stage) where they actually belong. Fixed a repo-wide bug where `notes` was byte-identical (copy-pasted) across every problem sharing a legacy pattern, misleadingly implying problem-specific content; relabeled as shared technique notes instead.

Added 50 supplemental problems to close 3 identified gaps — (1) new 'Complexity & Asymptotic Reasoning' module/Foundational stage (6 problems), (2) 8 new sorting-mechanics problems in Sorting & Interval Reasoning, (3) one additional problem for each of the 34 single-problem patterns across the bank, including extra Easy on-ramps for Graph and DP modules to soften difficulty cliffs.
